title: 3 Managing Your Central 1 Current Accounts
body_text:
3	Managing Your Central 1 Current Accounts
3.1	Funding a Central 1 Current Account
You can fund your Central 1 current account in the following ways:
Existing sources (e.g., cheques deposited by account holders)
Incoming AFT file credits
Internal account transfers (from other accounts held at Central 1)
Incoming wires (from other financial institutions)
Purchasing currency from Central 1
Purchasing US dollars from other sources (e.g., purchasing US dollar drafts and depositing them to your US dollar current account)
Note: 	If you maintain a HISA at Central 1, see Section 7.3, Viewing, Funding and Redeeming Funds from a High Interest Savings Account (HISA) via Treasury Connect, for more information.
3.2	Financial Institutions File
Payments Canada provides an electronic directory containing information to facilitate the routing of payments to the appropriate branches of Payments Canada members and other deposit-taking institutions in Canada. This directory is the Financial Institutions File (FIF). See Section 1.8, Contacts and Websites, for information on how to obtain the FIF.
Payments Canada updates the FIF weekly to reflect additions and modifications. Your financial institution is responsible for keeping relevant account information updated, such as the opening or closing of branches, address and branch name changes, and any banking arrangements that affect the routing of payment items.
3.3	Updating Current Account Information
Your financial institution is responsible for updating relevant account information such as opening or closing of branches, address and branch name changes, and banking arrangements that affect the routing of payment items.
To manage the Central 1 current account information for your branches, complete Central 1 Account Request, Form 3571. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Central 1 Account Request, Form 3571
Ensure that two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Forward the form to Central 1 as directed.
Retain a copy of the form for your records.
Related Information
Central 1 Account Request,
Form 3571
3.4	Stop Payment Requests on Primary Accounts
If your financial institution issues cheques on a primary account, you can request a stop payment on those items through ServiceNow. If the item hasnât cleared, Central 1 will place a stop payment on the item and post the service charge to your financial institutionâs current account.
Note:	Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. Other financial institutions can contact their Central 1 relationship manager.
